Title: Engineering triterpene metabolism in the oilseed of Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Squalene synthase-like (SSL) fusion enzyme derived from SSL-1 and SSL-3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts farnesyl diphosphate-derived prenyl units into the linear triterpene botryococcene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic seed-specific expression; minor stochastic ectopic expression detected in some leaves
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol or plastid depending on construct; plastid-targeted versions used Arabidopsis RUBISCO small subunit transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial and medicinal value as a hydrocarbon triterpene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled substantial botryococcene accumulation in Arabidopsis seeds; accumulation was high in both cytosol and plastid, with highest average levels in plastids when co-expressed with tpFPS and tpDXS, while one cytosolic line exceeded 1000 µg/g seed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic overexpression; subcellular targeting; gene stacking with FPS and DXS; GC-MS/GC-FID metabolite profiling in T3/T4 seeds and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific engineering under standard growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear direct correlation between botryococcene accumulation and seed weight; some plastid-targeted lines showed reduced seed weight; BS-only cytosolic line showed slight reduction in total phytosterols
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful direct product-forming gene for engineering non-native triterpene accumulation in oilseeds; stacking with FPS and plastid-targeted DXS improves yield; plastid targeting gives highest average production, whereas cytosolic targeting can achieve very high individual-line production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BS construct was a fusion of B. braunii SSL-1 and SSL-3 coding sequences; low botryococcene in tpBS-only lines was interpreted as possible mis-targeting or very low plastid FPP availability
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BS expression alone was sufficient to produce botryococcene in Arabidopsis seeds. 2. Co-expression with FPS increased average or maximal botryococcene accumulation, depending on compartment. 3. Plastid-targeted BS with tpFPS and tpDXS gave the highest average botryococcene accumulation. 4. Cytosolic BS supported strong accumulation of the non-native triterpene without major sterol increases.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Squalene synthase; C-terminal truncated soluble form
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two farnesyl diphosphate molecules to form squalene
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal/product-forming step and sterol precursor-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol or plastid depending on construct; engineered form was soluble due to deletion of native membrane-spanning domain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial and medicinal value; native precursor to phytosterols and other triterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Substantial squalene accumulation occurred only when plastid-targeted and co-expressed with tpFPS, with highest average levels further increased by tpDXS; cytosolic targeting produced only marginal accumulation beyond wild type
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic overexpression; plastid transit peptide targeting; co-expression with FPS and DXS; GC-MS/GC-FID metabolite profiling; sterol profiling in T4 seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific engineering under standard growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some plastid-targeted FPS-containing lines had reduced seed weight; cytosolic squalene likely underwent further metabolism and did not accumulate strongly
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising for plastid-based engineering of squalene in oilseeds, especially with FPS and DXS stacking; cytosolic deployment of soluble yeast SQS appears ineffective for accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The S. cerevisiae SQS used was C-terminally truncated and soluble, potentially limiting interaction with native Arabidopsis ER sterol machinery; only one cytosolic event showed modest sterol elevation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SQS alone or cytosolic SQS+FPS did not support major squalene accumulation in seeds. 2. Plastid-targeted SQS required co-expressed tpFPS for substantial squalene production. 3. Addition of tpDXS approximately doubled plastidic squalene accumulation. 4. Cytosolic soluble yeast SQS may be metabolically integrated poorly with native sterol biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: FPP supply module for triterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes farnesyl diphosphate from isopentenyl diphosphate and dimethylallyl diphosphate-derived prenyl intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enabling step for plastidic triterpene production in this study
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol or plastid depending on construct
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial and medicinal hydrocarbon triterpenes/sterol precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-expression of FPS was required for strong plastidic accumulation of both botryococcene and squalene and increased cytosolic botryococcene on average or in maximal lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene stacking with BS or SQS in seed-specific transgenics; compartment targeting; metabolite profiling of triterpenes and sterols
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific engineering under standard growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Presence of plastid-targeted FPS was associated with more frequent reduced seed weight in plastid-engineered lines; cytosolic FPS plus BS did not reduce total phytosterols relative to wild type
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential precursor-supply engineering component for triterpene accumulation, particularly in plastids where endogenous FPP is low; should be stacked with product synthases and possibly DXS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Very low endogenous plastid FPP explains dependence of plastid-targeted BS/SQS constructs on co-expressed FPS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FPS is a critical precursor-supply gene for engineered triterpene production. 2. Plastid-targeted BS or SQS required tpFPS for substantial product accumulation. 3. Cytosolic FPS improved the maximum capacity for botryococcene production. 4. FPS-containing plastid constructs, especially with tpDXS, gave the strongest overall yields.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: MEP pathway entry/precursor flux control step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of 1-deoxy-D-xylulose 5-phosphate from pyruvate and glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a putative rate-limiting step in MEP-pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Industrial and medicinal hydrocarbon triterpenes/sterol precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Addition of plastid-targeted DXS increased both botryococcene and squalene accumulation in plastid-engineered seeds by slightly more than twofold on average
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene stacking in plastid-targeted constructs; comparison of tpBS+tpFPS vs tpBS+tpFPS+tpDXS and tpSQS+tpFPS vs tpSQS+tpFPS+tpDXS; seed metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific engineering under standard growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Plastid-targeted engineering strategies including precursor-supply modules were more often associated with reduced seed weight, though crude seed oil percentage was unchanged
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for boosting plastidial precursor flux in seed metabolic engineering; should be stacked with plastid-targeted FPS and product synthases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The E. coli DXS gene was codon optimized for plant expression; paper cites DXS as potentially rate-limiting for MEP flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXS boosted plastidic accumulation of both engineered triterpenes. 2. Inclusion of tpDXS increased target product titres by a little over twofold. 3. Highest average yields for both botryococcene and squalene were obtained in tpBS/tpSQS plus tpFPS plus tpDXS lines. 4. Results support DXS as an important control point for precursor flux into plastid-engineered triterpene biosynthesis.
